_comment: Source of truth for the seeded demo space (docs/product/demo-script.md).
Human-editable. Edit this, then re-run:
cargo run -p riot-core --features conformance --example pack_demo_space
which rewrites demo-space.riot-evidence. The drift guard
(crates/riot-core/tests/demo_fixture_drift.rs) fails if you forget.
EVERY time here is a fixed constant. Nothing in the packer reads a clock:
the bundle must be byte-for-byte reproducible or the drift guard is a lie.
Seeds are fixed too, so each person's subspace id — and therefore the key
tag shown beside their name — is stable across rebuilds. Ana's seed was
ground so her tag begins a3f9, which is the tag the demo script reads out.

space.title: Riverside Tenants Union
_comment_organizer_secret_seed: The founding collective's subspace SECRET. Its corresponding subspace
public key IS the namespace id (organizer-shaped), so every member
derives the recognized-organizer coordinate from the space itself. The
seed was ground so that subspace key is communal (even final byte),
which a namespace id must be. Derived namespace/organizer subspace id:

_comment: The organizer signs the app Trust markers and the newswire
SpaceDescriptor. It is the space's founding authority, not a person on
the board, so it carries no profile card — it never renders as a member.
The open newswire the demo Home reads (Units 1A/2A). The organizer signs
one SpaceDescriptorV1 naming the founding editorial roster; members write
ordinary open-wire posts. This is additive to the six alerts, which still
back the incident board and sync.
newswire.summary: Neighbors keeping each other posted — court support, supply drops, and know-your-rights, published by the people who show up.

posts.author: ana
posts.headline: Hearing support is holding — thank you
posts.body: Every seat in the gallery was filled this morning, and the judge noticed. Same plan Thursday: gather at the laundromat by 8:25, bring a folding chair if you can.

posts.author: marcus
posts.headline: Supply drop restocked — formula and sizes 4–5 back in
posts.body: The Elm St lot table is full again after Saturday. Take what you need, no sign-in. If you can spare a pack, leave it and we'll pass it on.

alerts.author: ana
alerts.headline: Court support needed Thursday, 9am — bring folding chairs
alerts.description: Eviction hearings start at 9am on the third floor. The gallery has almost no seating, so bring a folding chair if you have one. Come for as long as you can; even twenty minutes in the room helps.

alerts.author: marcus
alerts.headline: Diaper and formula drop at the Elm St lot, Saturday 11–2
alerts.description: Sizes 1 through 5 and two brands of formula. Take what you need, no questions and no sign-in sheet. If you have extra to give, bring it by and we will pass it along.

alerts.author: priya
alerts.headline: Reminder: you don't have to open the door for anyone without a warrant
alerts.description: You can talk through the closed door. Ask them to hold any paperwork up to the peephole or slide it under. If they say they have a warrant, ask to read it before you open anything.

alerts.author: dee
alerts.headline: Ride offered to Thursday's hearing — 3 seats, leaving the laundromat at 8:30
alerts.description: Grey hatchback, out front of the laundromat on Vine. I can take three people there and back. Find me here or just be outside at 8:25 — I would rather wait than leave anybody.

alerts.author: ana
alerts.headline: Tuesday's general meeting moved to 7pm, same room
alerts.description: The community room was double-booked at six, so we start an hour later. Same room, same agenda, and we will still be out by half past eight.

alerts.author: marcus
alerts.headline: Found a ring of keys on the courtyard bench — come by unit 4B
alerts.description: Four keys and a small blue carabiner. Knock any evening this week and describe them and they are yours.

app._comment: The Shift Signup app of demo-script Beats 2 and 3. A real manifest+bundle pair — it goes through verify_app_pair like any other app. The permissions are read out loud on stage, verbatim.
app.author: priya
app.name: Shift Signup
app.description: Sign up for shifts at a court date, a supply drop, or a phone bank. Shows who has taken which slot, and lets you take one or give it back.
permissions: Can read the shift schedule board

resources.text:
Shifts

No shifts posted yet.

endorsements._comment: Demo-script Beat 2: 'Endorsed by Eastside Tenant Council and Courtyard Mutual Aid.'
endorsements.note: We ran two court dates off this.
Used it for the Saturday supply drops.

_comment: The built-in checklist app (fixtures/apps/checklist), half done.
app_id is content-derived: the packer re-derives it from the committed
starter catalog and fails loudly if this pin has gone stale.
Items store updated_by_id — an id, never a name snapshot (commit 26e45e7).
'Bring folding chairs' is deliberately UNCHECKED: it is the item Ana
checks live on stage in Beat 4.

items.author: ana
items.text: Confirm the hearing time with the court clerk

items.author: priya
items.text: Print 20 know-your-rights cards for the gallery
